title: Fixing Invalid Git Ref Name Validation

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate a Git ref name. A valid ref name consists of alphanumeric characters, dots, hyphens, and underscores. The name should not start or end with a dot, dash, or underscore. The name length should be between 1 and 63 characters. The function should return a boolean indicating whether the ref name is valid or not.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Git ref name
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the ref name is valid or not
statement.notes: Note that the function should handle edge cases such as empty strings, dots, dashes, and underscores at the start or end of the ref name.

examples.explanation: The ref name is valid according to the given rules
The ref name starts with a dot, which is not allowed
The ref name ends with a dot, which is not allowed
The ref name contains an invalid character, which is not allowed

hints.text: Check the length of the ref name and ensure it is between 1 and 63 characters
Check if the ref name starts or ends with a dot, dash, or underscore
Check if the ref name contains any invalid characters
